comments.text: A correction: our result [TaTe19] shows that the natural density is $1/2$ for almost all scales, rather than $0$ for almost all scales. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)

In "Xiaodong Lü, Zhiwei Wang. On the largest prime factors of consecutive integers. 2018. ffhal01797939v1f", the authors have proved that the density is larger than 0.2017. This is the best I could find at the moment... Here is the link to it. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)

This conjecture including two other conjectures of Erdos have been proved conditionally under the "Elliott-Halberstam conjecture for friable integers" in the paper "Three conjectures on P+(n) and P+(n + 1) hold under the Elliott-Halberstam conjecture for friable integers, 2021" of Zhiwei Wang. Here is the link to it: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0022314X21000196 Furthermore, the paper tells about the improvements on this problem pretty well too... (The site has been updated to address this comment.)

In Theorem 1.14 of this paper, Teräväinen proves a weaker version of [928] with logarithmic density instead of natural density. (Thanks to Agustín Meza for the reference.) As a consequence, in Theorem 1.17, Teräväinen obtains a formula for a logarithmic density for this problem. If [928] is true for natural density, then the solution for this problem should be as well. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
